LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFFS CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO, MAMIE HOW, RITA JONES, LEE SMITH AND GARY TOM'S REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION- MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THEIR DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGING THAT RESPONDENT'S DEPRIVATION OF THE RETIREMENT BENEFITS WITHOUT A HEARING VIOLATED PLAINTIFFS' DUE PROCESS RIGHTS. SEE PETRILLO V. BART (1988) 197 CAL.APP.3D 798, 808; GOLDBERG V. KELLY (1970) 397 U.S. 254; SKELLY V. STATE PERSONNEL BD. (1975) 15 CAL.3D 194. THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS WEIGHS IN PLAINTIFFS' FAVOR. THE PLAINTIFFS RELY ON THE BENEFITS TO SUPPORT THEMSELVES. IF RESPONDENT ULTIMATELY PREVAILS, THEN IT CAN EASILY RECOUP THE OVERPAYMENTS. THE MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED AS MOOT. THE COURT WILL HOLD OFF SIGNING THE ORDER UNTIL MS. MYERS SEND THE COURT BY WAY OF E-MAIL REGARDING THE STATUTE WHICH PERTAINS TO INTEREST. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT N. KIVEL, TEL. NO. (858) 459-3300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BEVERLY R. MYERS, OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5545 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFFS CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO, MAMIE HOW, RITA JONES, LEE SMITH AND GARY TOM'S Request For Preliminary injunction- Motion for Preliminary Injunction is granted. Plaintiffs are likely to prevail on their declaratory relief cause of action alleging that respondent's deprivation of the retirement benefits without a hearing violated plaintiffs' due process rights. See Petrillo v. BART (1988) 197 Cal.App.3d 798, 808; Goldberg v. Kelly (1970) 397 U.S. 254; Skelly v. State Personnel Bd. (1975) 15 Cal.3d 194. The balance of equities and harms weighs in plaintiffs' favor. The plaintiffs rely on the benefits to support themselves. If respondent ultimately prevails, then it can easily recoup the overpayments. The motion for writ of mandate is denied as moot. The court will hold off signing the order until Ms. Myers send the court by way of e-mail regarding the statute which pertains to interest. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
